# KitchenSync Touch partition table — 16: # slots take the same image from 87% to 27%.
# nvs and otadata keep the EXACT offsets of the Arduino default table (0x9000 / 0xe000)
# so an OTA update preserves saved WiFi + config. (A full flash via the web flasher
# erases NVS regardless -- that is the flasher's contract
# WHY THIS MUST SHIP BEFORE ANY UNIT LEAVES THE BENCH: a partition table cannot be
# changed over OTA. It can only be written over USB. A device handed to a customer on
# the old 4 MB table can never move to this one remotely -- so the window to fix it
# closes the moment hardware leaves the building.
# 0x10000 + 2*0x400000 = 0x810000 used by code; the rest is spiffs + coredump
# exactly at 16 MB. App slots stay 64 KB-aligned.
